summary: VAPT Security Hardening Testing Complete - All 30 security tests passed. Verified: security headers (8), Swagger disabled (3), error handling (2), JWT security (3), normal flows (2), authenticated endpoints (2), XSS sanitization (2), file upload security (4), NoSQL injection blocking (2), input validation (1), rate limiting (1).
critical_code_review_comments: All 8 OWASP security headers correctly implemented: X-Content-Type-Options, X-Frame-Options, X-XSS-Protection, Referrer-Policy, Strict-Transport-Security, Content-Security-Policy, Permissions-Policy, Cache-Control
Rate limiting working: 5 login attempts/min, 3 registrations/min, 5 avatar uploads/min - returns clean 429 message
NoSQL injection blocked: Pydantic rejects dict types, validation_exception_handler returns clean 422
XSS sanitization working: script tags and event handlers stripped from profile inputs
File upload security: validates magic bytes (not just extension/MIME), rejects >5MB, rejects wrong extensions
JWT hardened: 8-hour expiry (not 7 days), proper 401 responses for invalid/missing/expired tokens
password_hash never exposed in any API response (login, /me, profile update)
Error handling: no stack traces or internal details leaked in error responses
API root minimal: returns only {"status": "ok"}, no version info
context_for_next_testing_agent: VAPT security hardening fully tested. All security features verified: 1) Security headers on all responses, 2) Rate limiting (5/min login, 3/min registration, 5/min avatar), 3) NoSQL injection returns clean 422, 4) XSS stripped from inputs, 5) File uploads validated with magic bytes, 6) JWT 8h expiry, 7) No password_hash exposure, 8) Swagger/OpenAPI disabled, 9) Clean error messages. Note: Rate limiting is per-IP, so tests may hit 429 when run in quick succession - this is expected behavior.

rate_limiting.clean_message: VERIFIED - {"detail": "Too many requests. Please try again later."}

error_handling.validation_errors: VERIFIED - clean message, no internal details

mocked_apis.note: All tests against real API - no mocking
